reasoning: Katie Ledecky has historically dominated the 800m freestyle, holding the world record since 2013. In May 2025, she further lowered her own WR to 8:04.12. The closest any other swimmer has ever come is Summer McIntosh at 8:09.86, leaving a massive 5+ second gap. Even in the highly unlikely event that Ledecky's newest record was annulled on a technicality, her previous 8:04.79 record from 2016 would still make her the record holder. The only way she loses the record is if another swimmer beats 8:04.12. The resolution dates fall between late December 2025 and March 2026, which is typically short-course season and early long-course season, with no major taper events like World Championships or Olympic Trials where monumental 5-second WR progressions usually happen. Thus, the probability of someone breaking the record during this 3-month window is exceptionally low. Given the constraints to predict between 0.05 and 0.95 and to scale uncertainty with time, probabilities are set at the upper bound, slightly decreasing for later dates.
